title: Key Bindings
description: Learn how to create custom keybindings for quick command execution

sections.title: How Keybindings Work
sections.content:
Keybindings let you map keyboard shortcuts to commands. When you press a bound key, the associated command is automatically sent to the MUD server.

Keybindings are processed before your command reaches the input line, making them instant shortcuts.

sections.title: Creating a Keybinding
sections.content:
To create a new keybinding:

1. Go to Connection Settings
2. Navigate to Key Bindings
3. Click 'Add Keybinding'
4. Press the key combination you want to use
5. Enter the command to send
6. Click Save

Example: Press F1 to send 'look'

sections.title: Default Keybindings
sections.content:
MUDPuppy includes some default keybindings:

| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| F1 | look | Look around |
| Ctrl+F1 | help | Request in-game help |
| F2 | score | View your character status |
| F3 | inventory | Check your inventory |
| F4 | who | See who's online |

These can be modified or removed in Key Bindings settings.

sections.title: Special Keys
sections.content:
You can bind:

- **Function keys** (F1-F12)
- **Ctrl+letter** combinations (Ctrl+A, Ctrl+B, etc.)
- **Alt+letter** combinations
- **Arrow keys** (Up, Down, Left, Right)
- **Numpad keys** (NumPad0-NumPad9, NumPadEnter, etc.)

Note: Some key combinations are reserved by your browser and cannot be used.

**Numpad Note:** The numpad keys are detected as distinct from regular number keys, so you can bind both separately.

sections.title: Conflict Warnings
sections.content:
When creating a keybinding, you'll see a warning if:

- The key combination conflicts with browser shortcuts
- The key combination conflicts with another keybinding

Common conflicts:
- Ctrl+T (new tab)
- Ctrl+W (close tab)
- Ctrl+N (new window)
- F5 (refresh)

Choose a different key if you see a conflict warning.

sections.title: Editing and Deleting
sections.content:
To modify a keybinding:

1. Go to Connection Settings → Key Bindings
2. Click on the keybinding you want to edit
3. Modify the command
4. Click Save

To delete a keybinding:
1. Click the delete icon next to the keybinding
2. Confirm the deletion
